steps.title: Separate the clauses
steps.content: The sentence joins two independent clauses: one says technological change is generally linear, and the next says renewed interest in steam engines shows obsolete technologies can return.

steps.title: Punctuate even so
steps.content: Choice A puts the semicolon after the transition. Choice B omits the needed semicolon. Choice D uses only commas, creating a comma splice. A conjunctive adverb between independent clauses needs a semicolon before it and a comma after it. The answer is C.
